body.title: First page object: Gmail login — Igor proposes, CC reviews, iterate
body.result: Withdrawn per Akien 2026-05-03: workflow-first reshape (T-gmail-flow-layer) means pages get added LAST. Replaced by the new T-gmail-flow-layer scope; page object falls out of that.
body.description:
First real SWADL artifact. The point isn't the page object — it's the *reasoning trail* Igor builds while producing it. CC is pair-programmer, not code source.

## Pedagogical protocol
1. CC shows Igor the Gmail login URL + a SWADL page-object template
2. Igor proposes selectors (with his reasoning about *why* those selectors — stable attributes? aria-labels? data-testid?)
3. CC critiques — not rewrites. Asks 'what happens if Google A/B tests this element?'
4. Igor revises; deposits the finished page object + the reasoning as ARCHITECTURE_DECISION memory nodes
5. Test: run it headless, confirm it reaches the password prompt (do NOT submit real creds yet)

## What lives where
- page object code → wild_igor/tools/swadl_pages/gmail_login.py
- reasoning → ARCHITECTURE_DECISION nodes linked to PAGE_OBJECT + SELECTOR concept nodes (Hebbian: concepts activate when Igor looks at his own code)

## Non-goals
- Actually logging in with real credentials (next ticket)
- Two-factor flow (after login works)

## Related
- Parent: T-igor-as-user-epic
- Depends on: T-swadl-concepts-in-graph
- Unblocks: T-gmail-flow-layer

**Affected files:** wild_igor/tools/swadl_pages/gmail_login.py (new page object); tests/test_swadl_pages_gmail_login.py.

**Design rules:** docs-in-code (selectors carry rationale); test-plan-or-why-not (page-object tests against test Gmail account or local fixture HTML).

**Scope boundary:** IN — first page object; selectors + locators + reasoning trail. OUT — flow layer (separate T-gmail-flow-layer); production Gmail use; multi-step login (just the login page).

**Test plan:** tests/test_swadl_pages_gmail_login.py — assert selectors resolve against fixture HTML; document each selector's reasoning. E2E test deferred (requires test account).
